mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable demand expansion, yielding positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation above a 0.4pp threshold forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when inflation exceeds the threshold for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and declining unemployment validate a labor-market-driven consumption expansion, reducing recession probabilities and lifting equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signals produce negative Rank IC over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals supply-side capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate margins, generating immediate positive equity returns during periods of constrained supply.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently correlate with negative 5-day forward returns across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of aggregate demand weakness, triggering equity sell-offs as investors price in lower future earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in >50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint rise of short-term policy rates and core inflation signals real rate normalization that compresses equity multiples, producing negative forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Simultaneous FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll additions reflect resilient consumer spending power, providing a persistent upward drift in equity valuations over the weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no significant correlation with rolling 3-month PAYEMS growth trends.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in retail sales forecasts indicates weakening consumer demand, prompting immediate equity repricing as forward earnings estimates are revised downward over the next month.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines do not lead to negative 20-day forward returns in the majority of observed instances.
